title: Objectively Bad
description: A collection of great mods in a bad modpack making minecraft a terrible experience with quests included!
body:
# Pain and suffering in your local modrinth!

**do you like lightweight modpacks with a robust selection of unique mods, and resources? forming an entertaining, and great combination of modding capability?**

Too bad

## In **"Objectively Bad"** we have:
- too much difficulty
- mods that break balance
- redundant features
- bad frame-rate
- silly mods that are funny bad
- mods that features that aren't necessarily helpful
- too many biomes
- too many dimensions
- UI clutter
- UI elements that overlap and collide
- terrible crosshair
  and so much more!

Get yourself involved with chaos and disorder of **"Objectivly Bad"!**

## A small note section
It's very likely this pack might crash or is just generally unstable (it has a lot of mods) so just be aware it **_WILL_** be a bumpy ride

A reminder that _none_ of these mods are "bad" this collection of them _is_. The point of this pack is not to claim anyone's mods or resources are bad but that one can certainly make a bad modpack.

there is no such thing as a bad mod, modding takes time and effort; even more effort that has ever gone into this project.
